Web Copywriting Explained
Tele-Course Series and Continuity Program
Launch Summary:
Site: http://WebCopyWritingExplained.com
Product: 8-week Copywriting Tele-Course Series + $197 per month
forced continuity
Launch Date: Tuesday February 20th, 2007
Launch Partners: Over 50... only 13 were active. Most sales came from
3 people (one of them being me).
FINAL RESULTS:
83 total sales @ $597 each... total of $49,551 in initial course sales.
48 forced continuity sales @ $197 monthly... $9,456 in first month
revenue
Retention on continuity so far (3 months) is about 70% (30% non-
renewals/refunds). 3 month average = $6,895 (35 ongoing members)
Next Steps:
+ Physical Version, selling via PPC and JV Webinars
+ "Biz Op" Version, for those who want to be copywriters for hire, will
duplicate much of this launch
+ Build up Continuity Program: More robust content, less personal
interaction.
Problems/Mistakes:
+ Next time will hire a JV/Affiliate Manager
+ More of a "rolling launch" approach would have yielded more sales
+ Didn't have a great upsell/downsell process. Might need to get a
programmer involved to make the system do what I want.
+ Would NOT do all the personal one-on-one consults. Had over 90%
participations. 83 people, each with 2 30-minute consults. YIKES.
Stealth Launch to My List
----------------------------------
Pre Launch Started: 	1-16-2007
Soft Launch to My List: 	1-23-2007
Results: 25 sales, $14,925
"Second Flight" of 25 Seats Released: 1-25-2007
Results: 25 more sales, $14,925
JV Launch
---------------
Pre Launch started: 2-6-2007

Subject: Have you read these four books?
2-15-2007
------------------------------------------
'Ray Edwards's Response Marketing Letter'
------------------------------------------
A FIVE-MINUTE WEEKLY READ that gives you
specific tips, tactics, and techniques for
improving the response rates of your
advertising, promotions, and marketing.
THIS LETTER EXISTS TO HELP YOU MAKE MORE
MONEY.
Thursday February 15, 2007
------------------------
*** In This Issue ***
=> 'Ray's Recommended Reading List 101'
=> 'Copywriting Videos Still Available'
------------------------
'Ray's Recommended Reading List 101'
If you're just starting your library of
copywriting books, there are four books that
I recommend to get you rolling. Each of them
is relatively short. The first two are easy
reads and the second two are a little bit
more difficult for modern readers to get
through, but they are worth the effort. Here
are my four recommended copywriting books:
Bob Bly's 'Copywriter's Handbook'. This
really is the gold standard that gives the
best overall explanation of what copywriting
is all about. It covers not only direct
response copywriting which, is primarily what
this blog is devoted to, but all sorts of
copywriting. Whether it's for radio,
brochures, or even business cards I think
you'll really get a lot of value out of Bob's
book.
Dan Kennedy's 'The Ultimate Sales Letter'.
He's one of the deans of copywriting... and
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 26 of 71 --

Page 24 of 24
this book is pure gold. Dan lays out a good
basic system for writing copy in this brief
and highly-readable book.
Claude Hopkins, 'Scientific Advertising'.
This is one of the classics of direct
marketing advertising, especially for
copywriters. It's a little bit tougher read
just because it was written not long after
the turn of the previous century, so the
language seems a bit archaic and a bit more
formal than what we're accustomed to, but the
principles stand clear and true as ever.
Eugene Schwartz's 'Breakthrough Advertising'.
Not the kind of 'light and easy' read you
find in the business and marketing books of
today, this volume will yield wealth to those
who study it. Gene managed to squeeze the
most 'content per paragraph' into his work I
think I have ever seen. Read it and see if
you don't agree. Yes, some of the examples
and copy seem dated, but like Hopkins' work
the principles remain true.
That ought to keep you busy for a few days!
------------------------
*** Quotation of the Week ***
'Every single one of us can do things that no
one else can do - can love things that no one
else can love. We are like violins. We can be
used for doorstops, or we can make music. You
know what to do.'
Barbara Sher

2-21-2007
Subject: <$firstname$>...contest scandal, 3 new winners
Hello <$firstname$>
It's your update from Ray, complete with an
embarrassing confession...
A few people were upset about the contest
where I gave away 3 'Award Passes' to the
copywriting program.
(By the way, seats are still available at
http://WebCopyWritingExplained.com )
The complaint from all these folks was the
same: I asked for entries that were 250 words
or less, and we chose winners that were
longer than that.
Even though there were only a few complaints,
I found this distressing.
I won't quote all the emails for you, but
here's a sample:
'I am very disappointed in your contest and I
am sure many other of the entries feel the
same way ... you have not followed your own
rule you set out for the contest 250 words no
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 32 of 71 --

Page 30 of 30
more... looks like totally unethical to me
and all the others, looks like a fast cash
grab on your part on the whole thing. I hope
you feel proud of your self'
Well, let me just say: I'm sorry.
I'm sorry I didn't stick to the 250 word
rule... and I'm sorry I imposed the rule to
begin with.
It was a stupid rule.
I write long copy for a living, and I should
know better.
If a client told to there was a 'word limit'
on their copy, I'd probably give them their
money back and send them elsewhere.
But...
That doesn't change the fact that a handful
of people were... uh... very displeased with
our choice of winners.
So I'm going to award three more passes.
These three are all within the 250 word
limit...
OUR THREE NEW WINNERS
1. James Johnson (169 words)
2. Mark Edward Duin (117 Words)
3. Sharon Deloy (90 words)
I will be in touch with each of you about
your Award Pass.
Why did I admit to this 'scandal' publicly?
Look, I could have argued my case.
There are valid reasons behind the choices we
originally made (those folks STILL get their
passes, so don't worry about that).
And yes, it cost me another 3 slots in my
program to do this (a real cost, because I
plan to sell this baby out, and that means I
have now given away a total of $3,582 in
revenue!).
But more important to me than a few thousand
dollars is...
...that you KNOW that I value integrity --
and I value the opinion of my customers,
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

In fact, for some Internet Marketers, writing for clients is the fastest,
easiest way to break free from their job and start their own online
business. If you choose that path, you can always transition from being
a writer-for-hire to just writing copy for your own businesses and
products.
Great News: It's Not That Tough
Copywriting is a skill that can be learned by just about anybody.
Even if you don't consider yourself a "writer".
Even if you didn't get good grades in English.
If you can write good basic English, you can learn the skill of
copywriting. Ever write a memo at work? A love letter to your sweety?
Then chances are you have what it takes to be a copywriter.
All you need now is someone to show you the proven formulas,
the hidden structures, and the time-tested tactics of sales copy.
And that's exactly what I'm going to do in this tele-series.
What The So-Called "Gurus"
Won't Tell You
Here's the "dirty little secret" most of the so-called gurus simply
won't tell you: they're almost all great copywriters.
Even though many of them may now hire others (like me, for instance)
to write their copy, every single one of them knows: it's the copy that
makes them rich.
That's why they're willing to pay big bucks to have their copy written. I
know - I get paid large sums of money to write copy for websites and
marketers.
My minimum fee for a new sales letter is $7,500 (that's not B.S. - it's
publicly posted on my copywriter-for-hire website).

How You Can Avoid The Same Fate
I estimate that maybe as many as 90% of all business people, ad
copywriters, speakers, salespeople, and media salespeople just don't
follow the AIDA formula. Amazing, but true.
Most business people, copywriters, and marketers get hung up on the
wrong things, like:
Making sure the site/letter/mailer/brochure looks slick and
"big time".
Looking impressive to their peers.
Talking about themselves ("we've been in business 20
years"... "we're the Number One company in our field"...).
Being "creative" or "funny" without knowing if that helps get them
to their goal.
Being "professional" while sacrificing sales and prospects.
Being lazy and using the same tired formula approach for every
piece of copy.
Writing the copy themselves without knowing what really
works in copy, and why.
And yet - with just a little bit of work, and perhaps some help from
someone who knows what they're doing, it could all be so different.
If you get nothing else out of reading this letter, I hope you'll get this...
...analyze every advertising and sales activity in your business, and ask
yourself: does this follow the AIDA sequence? If it doesn't - change
that activity or advertising, or get rid of it altogether.
It's possible that this alone will turn around your business.
What if the only thing between you a pile of money... is words?
Take this training that we're offering in Web Copywriting Explained, and
learn to turn your "words into wealth". You could be just one sales
letter away from financial freedom!
